howItWorks.intro: This electrical panel capacity calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Panel Capacity (kW) = Panel Amps x Voltage / 1000
Utilization % = Current Load / Panel Rating x 100
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What is the 80% rule for electrical panels?
faq.answer: NEC requires that continuous loads not exceed 80% of the breaker or panel rating. A 200A panel should carry no more than 160A of continuous load. This provides a safety margin for inrush currents.

faq.question: When should I upgrade my electrical panel?
faq.answer: Upgrade when your panel consistently runs above 75% capacity, when adding major appliances (EV charger, heat pump), or when your panel is over 25 years old with outdated breakers.
